verdict_reasoning: TMED9 is a p24 family cargo receptor that cycles between ER and Golgi, functioning in COPI/COPII-mediated vesicular transport. Its steady-state localization is the early secretory pathway (cis-Golgi/ERGIC). Checking contextual buckets: (1) cell_state_induced—no documented stress/activation-induced PM display; (2) tissue_restricted_surface—ubiquitously expressed, not lineage-restricted; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis—not lysosomal; (4) dual_localization—no credible PM pool reported in surface proteomics or flow; recently implicated in mutant protein aggregation routing but still endomembrane; (5) stable_surface_attachment—not a secreted protein. No therapeutic program targets surface TMED9.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Small PM pool via secretory transit is conceivable but not established as a stable accessible epitope.
